sentiment.label: risk_off
sentiment.reason_it: Il sentiment di mercato è decisamente risk-off oggi, con il VIX che sale del 13.16% a 31.05, livello che indica panico. Il DXY debole (-0.03%) e l'aumento dell'oro (+1.51% a $4559.90/oz) sono ulteriori indicatori di una maggiore avversione al rischio. I mercati azionari sono in rosso, con S&P 500 -1.67% e STOXX 600 -0.90%. Il report settimanale di BlackRock Investment Institute segnala una possibile escalation del conflitto in Medio Oriente, il che potrebbe portare a una maggiore volatilità sui mercati.
sentiment.reason_en: Market sentiment is strongly risk-off today, with VIX surging 13.16% to 31.05, indicating panic. Weak DXY (-0.03%) and rising gold (+1.51% to $4559.90/oz) further underscore increased risk aversion. Equity markets are in the red, with S&P 500 -1.67% and STOXX 600 -0.90%. BlackRock Investment Institute's weekly report warns of potential escalation in the Middle East conflict, which could lead to increased market volatility.

market_impact_summary.it: Il mercato azionario è sotto pressione, con S&P 500 -1.67% e STOXX 600 -0.90%. Il settore energetico è impattato dal conflitto in Medio Oriente, con il prezzo del Brent che scende -3.59% a $108.53. L'aumento del VIX (+13.16%) e la discesa dei rendimenti obbligazionari (TLT -0.55%) indicano una maggiore avversione al rischio. Il report di Goldman Sachs Insights segnala una possibile recessione negli USA nel caso di un prolungamento del conflitto.
market_impact_summary.en: The equity market is under pressure, with S&P 500 -1.67% and STOXX 600 -0.90%. The energy sector is impacted by the Middle East conflict, with Brent crude down -3.59% to $108.53. The increase in VIX (+13.16%) and decline in bond yields (TLT -0.55%) indicate increased risk aversion. Goldman Sachs Insights' report warns of a potential US recession if the conflict prolongs.

audio_script_it:
Buongiorno a tutti, benvenuti nel nostro briefing finanziario di oggi, 30 marzo 2026. Io sono il vostro conduttore e vi accompagnerò attraverso le principali notizie e analisi dei mercati tradizionali e digitali.

I mercati asiatici hanno chiuso la giornata con un sentiment risk-off. Il Nikkei ha perso il 2,79% chiudendo a 51.886, mentre lo Shanghai Composite ha guadagnato lo 0,24% a 3.923,24. L'Hang Seng di Hong Kong ha ceduto lo 0,72% a 24.772. Questo sentiment negativo si sta propagando anche nei mercati occidentali.

In Europa, lo STOXX 600 ha perso lo 0,90% a 57,05. Negli Usa, l'S&P 500 ha ceduto l'1,67% a 6.368,85. Il VIX, indice della volatilità, è salito del 13,16% a 31,05. Sul fronte delle materie prime, l'oro ha guadagnato l'1,51% a 4.559,90 dollari l'oncia, mentre il petrolio Brent è sceso del 3,59% a 108,53 dollari. I tassi di interesse continuano a salire, con il tasso decennale Usa che ha raggiunto il 4,44%.

La geopolitica continua a influenzare i mercati, con le tensioni tra Iran e Usa che stanno avendo un impatto significativo sui prezzi dell'energia e sui mercati finanziari. La BCE ha recentemente mantenuto i tassi di interesse al 2,00%. In questo contesto, il Fondo Monetario Internazionale ha espresso preoccupazioni riguardo alla crescita economica globale.

Con questo scenario in mente, passeremo ora al comparto digitale, dove analizzeremo le ultime novità e tendenze nel mondo delle criptovalute. Restate sintonizzati.

Passiamo ora al comparto degli asset digitali. Iniziamo con un deep dive su Bitcoin. La criptovaluta ha registrato un valore di $67,287, in aumento dell'1.16%. Tuttavia, gli ETF hanno registrato un deflusso di $-225.5M. 

Spostiamo l'attenzione sulle altcoin. Ethereum è salita a $2,044, con un aumento del 2.24%, mentre Solana ha raggiunto $83.44, in aumento dell'1.41%. Binance Coin ha registrato un valore di $616.15, con un aumento dello 0.56%.

Per quanto riguarda il sentiment, l'indice Fear & Greed è a 8, corrispondente a Estrema Paura. Questo livello di paura estrema potrebbe essere correlato ai recenti dati macroeconomici, come il tasso di disoccupazione USA al 4.4% e il PCE Core YoY al 3.1%. Tuttavia, è importante notare che questi dati non sono recenti e potrebbero non riflettere la situazione attuale.

Infine, alcune notizie dal mondo crypto: BNP Paribas ha aggiunto sei ETN su Bitcoin e Ether per i clienti retail in Francia, mentre la Fed potrebbe annunciare importanti decisioni già la prossima settimana.

Grazie per l'attenzione e a domani
audio_script_en:
Good morning and welcome to today's financial update.

Global sentiment is risk-off today, with investors digesting a mix of economic data and geopolitical tensions. Key data points include a decline in the Nikkei index by 2.79% overnight and a rise in the VIX index by 13.16% to 31.05, indicating increased market volatility.

In traditional markets, equities are under pressure. The S&P 500 closed at 6368.85, down 1.67% overnight, while the STOXX 600 declined by 0.90% to 57.05. The Dollar Index is slightly lower at 100.12, down 0.03%. The Euro to US Dollar exchange rate is at 1.15, down 0.25%. Commodities are seeing safe-haven demand, with Gold at $4559.90 per ounce, up 1.51%, and Brent crude oil at $108.53 per barrel, down 3.59%. Bond yields are higher, with the US 10-year yield at 4.44%, up 0.54%, and the TLT Bond USA 20Y at 85.64, down 0.55%.

Geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, are driving market concerns. The recent developments have led to fears of a broader conflict, which could impact global economic growth and inflation. The US Federal Reserve's rate decision and economic indicators, such as the PCE Core YoY at 3.1%, are being closely watched. In the Eurozone, the recent GDP growth rate was 0.2% Quarter over Quarter, and the ECB rate is at 2.00%. The global M2 liquidity continues to expand at $22.7 trillion, with a Year over Year increase of 4.9%. Upcoming data releases, including the US PCE and Eurozone CPI, are expected to provide further insights into the economic landscape.

Moving on to the digital assets space, let's take a look at the current state of the cryptocurrency market. 

Starting with Bitcoin, we see that the daily net inflow for BTC ETFs has turned negative, coming in at $-225.5M. From a technical standpoint, BTC is trading at $67,287, up 1.16% for the day. 

Let's pivot to the cryptocurrency markets and examine some of the major altcoins. Ethereum is trading at $2,044, up 2.24%, while Solana is at $83.44, up 1.41%. Binance Coin is also seeing gains, trading at $616.15, up 0.56%. 

In terms of market sentiment, the Fear & Greed Index is currently at 8, indicating extreme fear. This is a sign that investors are becoming increasingly cautious, which can often be a contrarian indicator. Historically, periods of extreme fear have been followed by market rebounds. 

It's worth noting that traditional market data, such as the PCE Core YoY and Non-Farm Payrolls, are becoming less relevant due to their age. However, the recent CPI and Core CPI data from the Eurozone and the upcoming ECB rate decision may have a more significant impact on the market. 

Recent news in the space includes BNP Paribas adding six Bitcoin and Ether ETNs for retail clients in France, which could be a significant development for the adoption of cryptocurrencies in traditional finance. 


Thanks for tuning in. We'll be back tomorrow with another market update. Until then, stay informed and stay ahead. A quick look at what's on the radar for tomorrow and we'll wrap up here. Have a great day and we'll catch you soon.

articles.title: Punish Russia for Helping Iran Target the U.S. Military
articles.snippet: In the second week of March, U.S. President Donald Trump insisted that Russian President Vladimir Putin wants to be “helpful” in the Middle East. More recently, in a spat with the E.U. foreign policy chief over America’s diplomatic stance on Russia and Ukraine, Secretary of State Marco Rubio reportedly snapped “We are doing the best we can to end the war” and indicated the United States would be happy to stop if Europe thought it could do better. Admittedly, in diplomacy it is sometimes wise to say the opposite of what you believe. As such, the best I can say The post Punish Russia for Helping Iran Target the U.S. Military appeared first on War on the Rocks .

articles.title: The realities of economic security
articles.snippet: The Peterson Institute for International Economics presents a high-level analytical discussion of the realities of economic security, examining real-world trade-offs and vulnerabilities in today's geopolitically fractured world. Anjali Bhatt, host of PIIE Insider Live, moderates a candid discussion with PIIE experts: Warwick McKibbin, who presents global macroeconomic scenarios of the impact of the Middle East conflict; Cullen Hendrix, who discusses vulnerabilities of the global economy to the disrupted supply of commodities including food, fuel, and industrial inputs; and Jacob Funk…

articles.title: Oil Set to Book Record Monthly Gain on Iran Crisis
articles.snippet: Oil prices are about to log their biggest monthly rise on record following Houthi attacks on Israel and President Trump’s statement that he wants to take Iran’s oil. The international benchmark was set for a 59% increase since the start of March, Reuters said. At the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $115.52, after topping $116 per barrel earlier in the day in Asia following the news of the Houthi strikes. West Texas Intermediate was trading at $101.04 per barrel. “The conflict is no longer concentrated in the Persian…

articles.title: Africa Could Emerge As The Biggest Winner In Iran War
articles.snippet: The ongoing Middle East conflict has upended global energy markets, cutting off supplies of approximately 8 million barrels of crude per day and 20% of liquefied natural gas (LNG). Brent crude has surged more than 50% to around $110/bbl since the conflict erupted in late February, while the U.S. stock market has lost nearly $4 trillion. Previously, we reported that Russia has emerged as the biggest winner of the war, with the conflict providing a strategic "economic lifeline" to Moscow by elevating oil prices, distracting Western allies from…

articles.title: Drillers See Triple-Digit Crude and Hit the Brakes
articles.snippet: Brent crude is trading over $100 per barrel, WTI has topped $90, but oil drillers in the world’s largest producer are cautious about their future plans. In fact, they are rather unhappy with the war in the Middle East, because it has made it harder to plan investments. On the face of it, everything is perfect, price-wise. WTI is trading much higher than what shale drillers need to be profitable. According to the latest Dallas Fed Energy Survey, the range of WTI profitable drilling price levels for the oil patch is between $62 per barrel for…

articles.title: Oil Climbs on Fears of Multi-Front Supply Shock
articles.snippet: Oil prices had another volatile start to the week before breaking out due to a combination of military escalation and diplomatic breakdown. At the time of writing, after climbing to $116 and then flash crashing to $114, Brent Crude was trading at $116.69, up 3.66%. Meanwhile, West Texas Intermediate had risen 3.18% to $102.80. Another weekend of escalation in the conflict started with an Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia on Saturday, wounding at least 15 U.S. service members and damaging key aerial refueling assets. The strike…

articles.title: Middle East War Reignites the Energy Transition
articles.snippet: The worst oil and gas supply shock in history has exposed the vulnerability of dependence on fossil fuel imports and is making renewables popular again. As governments scramble to contain the fallout from the energy shock, both in supply and prices, increased electrification in transportation and power generation is once again the talk of the town. As the war in the Middle East laid bare the shock of losing oil and gas supply, policymakers and analysts are once again considering the benefits of fossil fuel importers boosting the share of renewable…
